title: BTS REUNITES! around a table in the apartment they once shared to announce and d...
content:
BTS REUNITES! around a table in the apartment they once shared to announce and discuss the hiatus. “I hope you don’t see this as a negative thing and see that it’s a healthy plan. I just hope, since ARMY has been with us for nine or 10 years, that you could understand,” J-Hope added. The hiatus would also be necessary for the members to complete their mandatory mili- tary service, as is required for all able-bodied young men in South Korea. “We can’t tell you everything directly, and that’s very sad and difficult at times,” Jimin said. “But if you’d take our words as they are, that would be great.” In October 2022, BTS held their “Yet to Come” concert, which was attended by approximately 50,000 fans at Busan’s Asiad Main Stadium and was viewed 49 million times via the live broad- cast on fan platform Weverse. (The show was also the basis for the documentary of the same name, now streaming on multiple platforms.) It was a “goodbye,” it was a “see you later,” it was a “don’t forget about us.” Now, after nearly four years, BTS is back with a new album and a world tour—and ARMY has not forgotten. But before we look ahead to the group’s bright future, let’s catch up with what the seven members have been up to in the meantime.

Prior to the hiatus, the guys of BTS had released solo music in the form of “mixtapes,” BTS solo tracks, and K-drama and video game soundtracks. But the break would allow the members an unprec- edented degree of time and space to explore their artistic identities outside of the group. An inten- tionally staggered enlistment schedule also meant frequent releases of new content for fans. J-Hope was the first member to make the solo leap—literally. The rapper jumped out of a box and onto the stage in front of more than 100,000 peo- ple at Lollapalooza in Chicago in July 2022, mak- ing history as the first South Korean artist to head- line a major U.S. music festival. It happened only two weeks after J-Hope released his solo debut Jack in the Box.The 10-track album, which ex- plored a grittier side of J-Hope’s cheerful persona, was the backbone of his Lollapalooza set list. His second solo album,Hope on the Street Vol. 1.,was released in March 2024, while J-Hope was serving in the military.
